members.name: ApplyScale
members.summary: Multiplies the current scale by the [param ratio] vector.

members.name: GetAngleTo
members.summary: Returns the angle between the node and the [param point] in radians. See also [method look_at]. [url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/godotengine/godot-docs/master/img/node2d_get_angle_to.png]Illustration of the returned angle.[/url]

members.name: GetRelativeTransformToParent
members.summary: Returns the [Transform2D] relative to this node's parent.

members.name: GlobalTranslate
members.summary: Adds the [param offset] vector to the node's global position.

members.name: LookAt
members.summary: Rotates the node so that its local +X axis points towards the [param point], which is expected to use global coordinates. This method is a combination of both [method rotate] and [method get_angle_to]. [param point] should not be the same as the node's position, otherwise the node always looks to the right.

members.name: MoveLocalX
members.signature: public void MoveLocalX(float delta, bool scaled)
members.summary: Applies a local translation on the node's X axis with the amount specified in [param delta]. If [param scaled] is [code]false[/code], normalizes the movement to occur independently of the node's [member scale].

members.name: MoveLocalY
members.signature: public void MoveLocalY(float delta, bool scaled)
members.summary: Applies a local translation on the node's Y axis with the amount specified in [param delta]. If [param scaled] is [code]false[/code], normalizes the movement to occur independently of the node's [member scale].

members.name: Rotate
members.summary: Applies a rotation to the node, in radians, starting from its current rotation. This is equivalent to [code]rotation += radians[/code].

members.name: ToGlobal
members.summary: Transforms the provided local position into a position in global coordinate space. The input is expected to be local relative to the [Node2D] it is called on. e.g. Applying this method to the positions of child nodes will correctly transform their positions into the global coordinate space, but applying it to a node's own position will give an incorrect result, as it will incorporate the node's own transformation into its global position.

members.name: ToLocal
members.summary: Transforms the provided global position into a position in local coordinate space. The output will be local relative to the [Node2D] it is called on. e.g. It is appropriate for determining the positions of child nodes, but it is not appropriate for determining its own position relative to its parent.

members.name: Translate
members.summary: Translates the node by the given [param offset] in local coordinates. This is equivalent to [code]position += offset[/code].

members.name: GlobalPosition
members.summary: Global position. See also [member position].

members.name: GlobalRotation
members.summary: Global rotation in radians. See also [member rotation].

members.name: GlobalRotationDegrees
members.summary: Helper property to access [member global_rotation] in degrees instead of radians. See also [member rotation_degrees].

members.name: GlobalScale
members.summary: Global scale. See also [member scale].

members.name: GlobalSkew
members.summary: Global skew in radians. See also [member skew].

members.name: GlobalTransform
members.summary: Global [Transform2D]. See also [member transform].

members.name: Position
members.summary: Position, relative to the node's parent. See also [member global_position].

members.name: Rotation
members.summary: Rotation in radians, relative to the node's parent. See also [member global_rotation]. [b]Note:[/b] This property is edited in the inspector in degrees. If you want to use degrees in a script, use [member rotation_degrees].

members.name: RotationDegrees
members.summary: Helper property to access [member rotation] in degrees instead of radians. See also [member global_rotation_degrees].

members.name: Scale
members.summary: The node's scale, relative to the node's parent. Unscaled value: [code](1, 1)[/code]. See also [member global_scale]. [b]Note:[/b] Negative X scales in 2D are not decomposable from the transformation matrix. Due to the way scale is represented with transformation matrices in Godot, negative scales on the X axis will be changed to negative scales on the Y axis and a rotation of 180 degrees when decomposed.

members.name: Skew
members.summary: If set to a non-zero value, slants the node in one direction or another. This can be used for pseudo-3D effects. See also [member global_skew]. [b]Note:[/b] Skew is performed on the X axis only, and [i]between[/i] rotation and scaling. [b]Note:[/b] This property is edited in the inspector in degrees. If you want to use degrees in a script, use [code]skew = deg_to_rad(value_in_degrees)[/code].

members.name: Transform
members.summary: The node's [Transform2D], relative to the node's parent. See also [member global_transform].
